publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-03-11 23:02:46 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Kenneth G. Wilson papers
did.abstract: Professional papers of Kenneth G. Wilson.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Kenneth G. Wilson taught at Cornell from 1963 to 1987. In 1987, Wilson left Cornell for Ohio State University where he helped found the Physics Education Research Group, and focused on physics and science education. Wilson won the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1982.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Professional papers of Kenneth G. Wilson. Collection includes publications and notes for publications by Wilson, correspondence, material relating to Wilson's teaching and courses, data, computer printouts, and research notebooks. Items relate to his own work in particle physics, quantum field theory, supercomputing and the Cornell Theory Center, and education reform.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Selected Materials from Cornell Box 1
Series III. Correspondence and Memorabilia, Materials from Wilson's Physics Courses Box 3
Series IV. Wilson's Research Notebooks from Cornell Box 4
Series V. Raw Research Notes Box 6, 7
Series VII. Materials on Supercomputing and the Cornell Supercomputing Theory Center Box 6
Series VIII. Materials on Education Reform Box 7

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Kenneth G. Wilson Papers, #14-22-4086. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Selected Materials from Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material selected by Ken to take with him to the Dibner Institute for the History of Science and Technology (visited 2002)

did.unittitle: Gibbs' Lecture "Proof of a Conjecture by Dyson", CV

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Abstract of Gibbs Lecture, American Mathematical Society, 1980 reprint of early paper "Proof of a Conjecture by Dyson", several versions of Wilson's CV

did.unittitle: "Generalized Wannier Functions","Future of Supercomputing"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript and proof of "Generalized Wannier Functions" with notes, letter from Michael Teter (Corning Glass Laboratory) rough notes on "Future of Supercomputing"

did.unittitle: Monte Carlo Renormalization Group on FPS Supercomputer

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red folder: Implementation of Monte Carlo Renormalization Group transformations on the Floating Point Systems supercomputer

did.unittitle: Research notes on atomic physics, strong coupling lattice gauge theory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on atomic physics calculations, Floating Point systems implementation, rough notes for "Future of Supercomputing", Indian exercise book (Balarpur Industries) filled with strong coupling lattice gauge theory calculations (of vacuum energy?)

did.unittitle: Notes on quantum chemistry and supercomputing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on programming Pade approximants, notes on P, C, and T (for a class?), manuscript on quantum chemistry, press release on supercomputing

did.unittitle: Lecture and reference notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Quantization of the electromagnetic field, some QFT cross section formulae, notes on Johnson's paper on the Thirring model, 4-pt functions in the Thirring model, conformal group, generators of the Lorentz group, solution of the Ising model, high-temperature series calculations (Ising model?), correlation functions in the Ising model, "Schwinger methods" (correlation functions) in the Ising model, notes on Toeplitz determinants (for the Ising model), thermodynamics of a monatomic gas, logarithmic integrals, integrals over lattice momenta, lattice quantum field theory calculations, Gaussian integration, motion of a particle in slowly varying fields,

did.unittitle: Manuscripts of Unpublished Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Model for Coupling Constant Renormalization LNS-65-13 (1965) (7 pp), Renormalization Theory for Strong Interactions (1968-9?), about 100 pages of manuscript, perhaps to accompany "Nonlagrangian Models …" Also original manuscripts for the materials "selected for the Dibner Institute 2004"

did.unittitle: Rough notes on supercomputing, blockspin, instantons, atomic physics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rough notes on supercomputing, rough notes on block spin calculations and computer implementation, exchange of letters with Paulo Caldas, unpublished manuscript on instantons in lattice gauge theory, rough notes on atomic density matrix computations, rough notes on supercomputer revolution

did.unittitle: Rough notes on Wannier function, blockspin, FPS programing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rough notes on Wannier function calculations, notes on Dirac equation on a lattice, notes on block spin formalism, rough notes on Floating Point Systems programming, synchrotrons, very rough notes on atomic calculations, Cornell Theory Center, computing revolution, Ising simulations

did.unittitle: Research notes on atomic physics, fixed source problem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rough notes on atomic physics calculations (1970's), very rough notes for FPS programming, few pages of manuscript on operator products (1964?), notes on "topological anaysis" -- convergence of operator interation in a renormalization group transformation in the fixed source problem, notes on analysis of the fixed source problem

did.unittitle: Notes on topics in quantum field theory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Effective Hamiltonian in the fixed source problem, relation between scattering cross sections and the S-matrix, representations of the Lorentz group, Lorentz-invariant wave equations, relation between scattering cross sections and the S-matrix (version 2), chiral SU(3)xSU(3) symmetry, some current algebra formulae, representations of SU(3), Dirac equation in 1 dimension, Lorentz group commutators, 2nd order weak interactions (with Operator Product Expansion), Steinberger calculation of pi-zero decay, irreducible representations of the Lorentz group, also: manuscript for "Regge Roles and Multiple Production" (1963) (+loose notes on renormalization group transformations)

did.unittitle: Notes for Physics 574 - Applications of Quantum Mechanics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Looseleaf notebook w. problem sets and course notes (notes after the green separator might be by Tung-Mow Yan), after the green separator, quotes from Ken taken down by students: e.g. "If you do the calculation properly and come back a year later, you will find that it is still divergent…"

did.unittitle: Series II. Wilson's Publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged chronologically. Mainly, these are reprints, but in some cases, a manuscript or other correspondence is included. These cases are noted.

did.unittitle: Dispersion Relations for Infinite Plasmas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript from Wilson's graduate study at Caltech

did.unittitle: An Investigation of the Low Equation and the Chew-Mandelstam Equations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ph.D Thesis, Manuscript

did.unittitle: Regge Poles and Multiple Production, Acta Phys. Aust. 17, 37

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript located within Series I.

did.unittitle: Proof of a Conjecture by Dyson, J. Math. Phys. 3, 1040

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With a related news article from Science (2005)

did.unittitle: A Set of Double Integral Equations for Low-Energy Pion-Pion Scattering

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript from Harvard "to be submitted to Phys. Rev." (but apparently not submitted)

did.unittitle: Solution of the Symmetric Scalar Low Equation in the One Meson Approximation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript from Cornell

did.unittitle: A Model for Coupling Constant Renormalization

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: LNS-65-13 ; this unpublished manuscript is located in Series I

did.unittitle: On Products of Quantum Field Operators at Short Distances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript submitted to the Physical Review (1965) (and parts of an earlier draft), referee report (from Arthur Wightman), notes for an answer to the referee report the paper was never resubmitted. Wilson developed these ideas for many more years and announced them in his article "Nonlagrangian Models of Current Algebra" (1969)

did.unittitle: Renormalization Theory for Strong Interactions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished manuscript located in Series I

did.unittitle: Some Experiments on Multiple Production

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CLNS-131 ; eventually published in the proceedings of the 1973 Scottish Universities Summer School

did.unittitle: Renormalization Group and Strong Interactions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. D 3, 1818

did.unittitle: Renormalization Group and Critical Phenomena I. Renormalization Group and the Kadanoff Scaling Picture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. B 4, 3174

did.unittitle: Renormalization Group and Critical Phenomena II. Phase-Space Cell Analysis of Critical Behavior

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. B 4, 3184

did.unittitle: Deep Inelastic Scattering - Theory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Draft of the article for the proceedings of the 5th International Symposium on Electron and Photon Interactions at High Energies, Cornell

did.unittitle: Gauge Transformations in the Thirring Model

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. D 5, 1026

did.unittitle: Renormalization Group II

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished lectures given at Cornell, lecture notes by J. Serene

did.unittitle: Renormalization Group III

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unpublished lectures given at Cornell, lecture notes by J. Serene

did.unittitle: Breakdown of Landau Theory, Fluctuations and Renormalization

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offprint from the 1973 Nobel symposium proceedings containing an abstract of the talk and a record of the discussion

did.unittitle: Solution of the Spin-1/2 Kondo Hamiltonian

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Offprint from the 1973 Nobel symposium

did.unittitle: Quantum Field Theory Models in Less Than 4 Dimensions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. D 7, 2911

did.unittitle: The Renormalization Group and the Epsilon Expansion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Reports 12C

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. D 10, 2445

did.unittitle: Finite Lattice Approximation to Renormalization Groups

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. B 11, 3431

did.unittitle: Temperature-Dependent Susceptibility of the Symmetric Anderson Model: Connection to the Kondo Model

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Phys. Rev. Lett. 35, 1101

did.unittitle: Renormalization Group Methods

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Adv. Math. 16, 170

did.unittitle: The Renormalization Group and Block Spins

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Boltzmann Medal Address

did.unittitle: The Renormalization Group: Critical Phenomena and the Kondo Problem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rev. Mod. Phys 47, 773; with a folder of original artwork for this and contemporaneous papers

did.unittitle: Quark Confinement and Lattice Calculations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preprint of a talk that seems to honor Paul Dirac

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Phase Transitions and Critical Phenomena, v. 6, ed. by Domb and Green

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Problems in Physics with Many Scales of Length

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Technical Articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an article on Gaussian basis sets for atomic physics, the draft of a grant proposal for the Cornell Theory Center, an article on constituent quarks, section 1 -- Critical Phenomena (draft from a post-1983 paper) and other miscellaneous materials

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous Correspondence with Publishers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Physical Review, World Scientific

did.unittitle: Slides from Wilson's Talks
PhD Thesis Draft (Steven White?)
Series III. Correspondence and Memorabilia, Materials from Wilson's Physics Courses
Letters of recommendation and replies to requests for evaluation
Correspondence on history and philosophy of science

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten draft "Psychological Paradigms and Scientific Truth"

did.unittitle: Correspondence with politicians about science

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Congressional testimony

did.unittitle: Miscellaneous correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly replies to invitations

did.unittitle: Materials related to grant proposals at Cornell and Ohio State

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials specifically concerning the Cornell Theory Center are in Box # 8, folder #5

did.unittitle: Various papers sent to Wilson for comments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two sub-folders: "Awards Won by Ken - Correspondence" and "Awards Won by Ken - Information"

did.unittitle: Materials related to Wilson's awards
Materials related to Wilson's Nobel Prize award
Interviews at Roundtables

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Transcript of a roundtable on supercomputing, 1985, interview at Ohio State University, 2000, videotape of American Physical Society luncheon with Nobel laureates: "Celebrate a Century of Physics, Nobel Luncheon Version", 1999 (V-7155)

did.unittitle: In memory of E. Bright Wilson
Ken Wilson vs. Columbia State University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding a fraudulent university using Wilson's name

Series V. Raw Research Notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mainly from Ohio State; grouped as they were in the original boxes

did.unittitle: Notes from Cornell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mainly on computer implementations for light-front QCD

did.unittitle: Raw Notes-Mainly Atomic Physics Calculations
Raw Notes-Mainly Light-Front Hamiltonian Calculations
Ohio State Note Pads-Mainly Light-Front Hamiltonian
Notes from Ohio State-Mainly Light-Front QCD
Raw Notes-Mainly Science Education
Series VI. Computer Output

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous Computer Output from Cornell; includes report : "Simulating QCD with Dynamical Wilson and Staggered Fermions"

did.unittitle: Series VII. Materials on Supercomputing and the Cornell Supercomputing Theory Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documentation for the Gibbs Project

did.unittitle: Articles on Supercomputing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Theoretical Science and the Future of Large Scale Computing," "CERN Courier" (1983) manuscript, "Computing Support for Scientific Research," "Technology and Science of Informatics" (1983) page proofs and manuscript with corrections

did.unittitle: Drafts for Articles on Supercomputing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly undated; "Planning for the Future of U.S. Scientific and Engineering Computing", "Theoretical Science and Computer Technology", "Supercomputers: Market Size and Market Risk", "Role and Needs for Large Scale Computing for Theoretical Physics", "The Computerization of Science" (c. 1983), "Present vs. Future Supercomputers", "Supercomputers", "Theoretical Science in the Coming Decades", "DD Seminar: Theoretical Science and the Future of Large Scale Computing", "Supercomputers, Science, and the American Spirit", Ken Wilson's talk: "Questions and Answers", Topics for Talk by K. Wilson at Los Alamos Panel (1983)

did.unittitle: Formal Reports and Congressional Testimony

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memorandum to "Present and Potential Supercomputer Users" (April, 1981), Memorandum to "Users fo Large-Scale Computing in Government, Industry, and Universities" (July 15, 1981)(2 copies), "Supercomputers -- outline for presentation at meeting of Business-Higher Education Forum" (Jan. 22, 1983), letter to J. D. Kuehler, Sr. VP of IBM (April 9, 1984), presentation at the Truman Centennial (1984), testimony at the Supercomputer hearing of the House Science and Technology Committee (Tallahassie, August, 1985), transcript of the AFIPS 25th anniversary Policy Symposium (May, 1986), Meeting the Grand Challenges of Science at the National Supercomputer Centers, meeting of the center directors (at the NSF?) (February, 1987), White Paper on Advanced Computation and Simulation of Complex Material Phenomena (February, 1987), testimony before the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology (March, 1988), presentation to the National Governors' Association Working Group on State Initiatives in Applied Research (Washington, April, 1988), transcript of the executive seminar on Supercomputing for the Future of the United States (Boston University, June, 1989), transcript of a panel: Fiber Optics, an Important tool (undated; includes Gov. Mario Cuomo)

did.unittitle: Series VIII. Materials on Education Reform
A National Plan for Education Reform

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report, correspondence and responses

did.unittitle: Drafts of Papers on Education Reform

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Industrial Redesign", "Achieving Education Reform" (op. ed. piece) (2 copies), "Diagnosing Productivity Problems", "K-12 Scientific Literacy: a Plan for Ohio State University and the State of Ohio" (August 1989), "A Cognitive Studies/Teacher Enhancement Program at Ohio State University" (several stages of drafts), "Innovation and Basic Research: the Key to Achieving National Education Goals", "Briefing Paper: Achieving the National Goals for Education", "Operation Horace Mann", "Achieving Education Reform"

did.unittitle: On Peter Drucker's School Reform Proposals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drucker on Education: "Does Drucker have the best ideas for school reform?" (March 2006) "A New Strategy for Education Reform, Starting with a Skunk Works", "Reviving Drucker's Research Discipline of Social Ecology" (December 2007) (drafts - K. G. Wilson and C. Barsky)

did.unittitle: Substantive Correspondence on Education Issues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Exchange of letters, and related materials to/from: Arnold Arons (University of Washington), Lewis Branscomb (J. F. Kennedy School, Harvard), Urie Bronfbrenner (Cornell), Harvey Brooks (J. F. Kennedy School, Harvard), S. Cecchetti, Paul David (Stanford), Julie Dougherty, Louis Gerstner (Chairman and CEO, IBM), Z. Golab-Meyer (Jagiellonian University, Krakow), Kurt Gottfried (Cornell), Dee Hock (CEO emeritus, VISA), Paul Kennedy (Yale), Jack Keuhler (Vice Charman, IBM), Leon Lederman (Director emeritus, Fermilab), Mervin Muller (Ohio State), Sharon Porter Robinson (COO, Educational Testing Service), Seymour Sarason (Yale), Dick and Iris Schutz, Robert Slavin (Johns Hopkins), W. Rodman Snelling, Karim Joseph Mourad (Ohio State), John Staudenmaier (University of Detroit Mercy), Carl Wieman (University of Colorado; now at Stanford), Alexandra Wigdor (National Research Council), William Wulf (National Academy of Engineering)
